mandate: Lower middle market private equity — proprietary acquisition of founder-owned service businesses
excitement_signals: Growing market with secular tailwinds (aging population, pet ownership, cybersecurity mandates, etc.)
Limited direct competition in the local/regional market
Clear 'right to win' — something genuinely special about this company
Defensible moat: accreditation, certification, proprietary process, regulatory license, IP
Recurring or contractual revenue (wellness plans, subscriptions, retainers, managed services)
Multi-location operator with whitespace to expand
Essential/non-cyclical service people can't stop buying
Founder approaching retirement — natural succession opportunity
Fragmented market with no dominant player — ripe for buy-and-build
deal_breakers: Professional PE or VC investor on the cap table
Commodity service with no differentiation from competitors
Heavy customer concentration (>30% from one client)
conviction_bar: Only present companies where you can articulate a specific, compelling reason to be excited. 'Good company in a growing market' is not enough. What makes THIS one special? What is the unfair advantage?
